name: s3
context: Upload, download, and manage objects in Amazon S3 buckets
info: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service) provides scalable object storage with high availability and durability. Objects are stored in buckets, organized by keys (paths). S3 supports versioning, lifecycle policies, access control via bucket policies and ACLs, and presigned URLs for temporary access. The AWS SDK v3 `@aws-sdk/client-s3` package provides PutObject, GetObject, DeleteObject, ListObjects, and other commands. Use `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner` for generating presigned URLs. S3 integrates with [[aws/lambda]] for event-driven processing and [[aws/cloudformation]] for bucket provisioning.
body:
# Amazon S3

S3 is AWS's object storage service. Objects (files) live in buckets, addressed by a key (path). Every object has a unique combination of bucket + key. S3 delivers 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability and integrates deeply with other AWS services.

## Upload and Download

```javascript
import { S3Client, PutObjectCommand, GetObjectCommand } from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";

const s3 = new S3Client({ region: process.env.AWS_REGION });

// Upload
await s3.send(new PutObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket",
  Key: "path/to/file.txt",
  Body: Buffer.from("Hello, S3!"),
  ContentType: "text/plain",
}));

// Download
const { Body } = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket",
  Key: "path/to/file.txt",
}));
const content = await Body.transformToString();
```

## Presigned URLs

Generate time-limited URLs for secure access without sharing credentials:

```javascript
import { getSignedUrl } from "@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner";

const url = await getSignedUrl(s3, new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket",
  Key: "path/to/file.txt",
}), { expiresIn: 3600 }); // 1 hour
```

Presigned URLs are ideal for allowing clients to upload or download without direct AWS credentials.

## Bucket Policies

Bucket policies control access at the bucket level using JSON policy documents. Combine with [[aws/iam]] policies for fine-grained control:

- **Public read** — allow anonymous GET requests (use sparingly)
- **Cross-account** — grant access to IAM principals in other AWS accounts
- **IP restrictions** — limit access to specific CIDR ranges
- **VPC endpoint** — restrict access to traffic from a specific VPC

## Lifecycle Rules

Automate object transitions and expiration:

- **Transition** — move objects to cheaper storage classes (S3 Standard -> S3 Glacier) after N days
- **Expiration** — automatically delete objects after N days
- **Abort incomplete multipart uploads** — clean up failed large uploads

Lifecycle rules reduce storage costs for data with known access patterns. Define them in [[aws/cloudformation]] templates for infrastructure-as-code management.

## Storage Classes

| Class | Use Case | Durability |
|-------|----------|------------|
| S3 Standard | Frequently accessed data | 99.999999999% |
| S3 Intelligent-Tiering | Unknown access patterns | 99.999999999% |
| S3 Glacier | Archival, minutes-to-hours retrieval | 99.999999999% |
| S3 Glacier Deep Archive | Long-term archival, 12-hour retrieval | 99.999999999% |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | IAM access key |
| `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | IAM secret key |
| `AWS_REGION` | Target region for the S3 client |

sections.title: Upload and download
sections.body: Use `PutObjectCommand` to upload and `GetObjectCommand` to download. Specify Bucket, Key, and Body (for uploads). Set ContentType for proper MIME handling. For large files, use multipart upload via `@aws-sdk/lib-storage` Upload class. Downloads return a readable stream — use `Body.transformToString()` for text or pipe to a file.

sections.title: Presigned URLs
sections.body: Generate time-limited signed URLs with `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`. Set `expiresIn` in seconds (default 900, max 604800 for 7 days). Use presigned PUT URLs for client-side uploads without exposing credentials. Presigned URLs inherit the permissions of the IAM principal that generated them — see [[aws/iam#policies]] for scoping.

sections.title: Bucket policies and access control
sections.body: Bucket policies are JSON documents attached to buckets that grant or deny access. Combine with [[aws/iam]] user/role policies. Use `s3:GetObject`, `s3:PutObject`, `s3:DeleteObject` actions. Block public access by default — enable only when explicitly needed. Use S3 Block Public Access settings as a safety net.

sections.title: Lifecycle rules
sections.body: Automate storage class transitions and object expiration. Transition to Glacier after 90 days for archival. Expire temporary objects after 30 days. Abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days. Define lifecycle rules in [[aws/cloudformation]] templates or via the S3 console. Reduce costs by matching storage class to access patterns.
